spec_match.rationale: Uses real ::part(input):focus-visible and ::part(input):hover host-page rules against a genuine shadow-DOM custom element.

interactive.rationale: Real form fields, keyboard focus, and the pre-Chrome-130 fallback mode remain testable in-page.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page still matches the feature blurb by demonstrating a host-page focus ring on an exposed input part.

edge_cases.rationale: The page contrasts pre-130 and Chrome 130 modes, but it still does not probe combinators, :has(), or tree-structural selectors that remain rejected.

references.rationale: The ChromeStatus reference now points to the canonical feature id 5195333643272192.

design_system.rationale: The existing design-token based page styling remains intact.

openQuestions.title: Probe combinators and :has() rejection
openQuestions.detail: Add a third panel that runs CSS.supports() against still-rejected chains (form-field::part(input) > .x, form-field::part(input):has(.y), form-field::part(input):nth-child(2)) so the visitor leaves with a clear mental model of the new boundary, not just confirmation of what works.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate :focus-within at the label part
openQuestions.detail: The rejected-list teases form-field::part(label):focus-within but the page never applies it. Wire it up so the label restyles when the input is focused — that's a richer demo than just outlining the input itself.

summary: The concept continues to demonstrate host-page ::part():focus-visible styling on a real shadow component, and the wrong ChromeStatus anchor has been corrected.
